purpose: Implements the `!instrument` command: scans .jsx/.tsx/.html/.htm files in a target project and adds `data-synapse-label="<UUID>"` attributes to text-bearing elements (h1-h6, p, span, button, a, label, li, td, th, blockquote, dt, dd, plus a small set of common custom text components like TextAnimated, TextLoop, Marquee, StyledText, Link). The labels are used by Live Preview to map a clicked DOM element back to its source file. Also provides removeInstrumentation() to strip every label attribute. The hand-rolled parser handles JSX expression braces, quoted attributes (", ', `), HTML comments, JSX `{/* */}` comments, and skips child-component JSX expressions when checking whether a tag has direct text content.
gotchas: Skips node_modules, .next, dist, build, .git — but does NOT skip vendor/, .turbo/, .cache/, etc. Add new ignores manually if a new framework appears.
Self-closing tags like `` are skipped by design (no text content). Custom self-closing components emitting text via render-props will not get labels.

isInsideCommentOrString is a HEURISTIC — it can be fooled by `//` or `/* */` inside template literals that span multiple lines. Acceptable for the current use case, but don't rely on perfect accuracy.

hasDirectTextContent strips JSX expressions `{...}` AND child tags before checking for non-whitespace — so `
text
` will NOT instrument the outer div (correct), but it DOES instrument any tag with bare text + child components mixed in.

Each label is `crypto.randomUUID()` — re-running !instrument on already-labeled files only adds labels to NEW unlabeled tags (idempotent on existing labels). Removing then re-adding labels assigns NEW UUIDs, breaking any persisted label-to-source mapping.
TARGET_ELEMENTS is a hardcoded list. Tags not in this list (e.g., `<strong>`, `<em>`) are never instrumented — Live Preview cannot edit them.

Regex matches are case-insensitive (`gi`) but TARGET_ELEMENTS includes both lowercase `a` and PascalCase `Link`/`StyledText` — JSX is case-sensitive in real React, but the matcher accepts both, which can over-match HTML pages that happen to have `<Link>` text.
findTagClose tracks brace depth to skip `>` inside JSX expressions, but it does NOT detect TypeScript generics like `<Component<Foo>>` — those would confuse the parser (rare in attributes).
patterns: Two-pass instrumentation: (1) collect all valid tag matches with their attribute slices, (2) rebuild output string by walking matches and inserting ` data-synapse-label="..."` right before the closing `>`.
Hand-rolled JSX-aware tag closer (findTagClose) instead of using a JSX/babel parser — keeps the dependency surface tiny but means tricky edge cases (TS generics, escaped templates) are unsupported.
Idempotent: existing data-synapse-label attributes are detected and skipped via simple substring search of the attrs slice.
conventions: Errors are accumulated per-file in an `errors[]` array rather than throwing.
Synchronous fs API — runs once at command time, blocking is acceptable.
